name: Red-osier Dogwood
summary: Brilliant red winter stems — visible across the brown winter landscape from a quarter mile away. Wet meadows, swale margins, and stream banks. Hosts 120+ Lepidoptera species including Spring Azure.
notes: White flower clusters in May–June; white berries in August feed 30+ bird species. Forms thickets — excellent bank stabilizer. Trivially propagates from live stakes cut in late winter and pushed into wet ground. Functional fill for wetter sites than gray dogwood already on the list.

planting.aspect: Wet meadows; stream banks; swale margins
planting.soil: Tolerates clay, sand, organic; pH adaptable
planting.site_notes: Propagate cheaply from live stakes — cut 12–18 inch sections of dormant 1-year wood in late winter, push into wet ground, water once. No deer protection needed; light browse only. Plant in colonies of 5+ for thicket effect and winter color mass.
